metadata.method: hand-drafted, 4-pillar lens, every candidate alias pre-measured for Colombia-share inside AMERICAS-ANDEAN
notes: Gate is AMERICAS-ANDEAN, which also covers EC/BO/PE/GY, so each alias was measured for what share of its ANDEAN hits are actually Colombian before being kept.

KEPT with measured Colombia-share: 'White House'/'Casa Blanca' 27/29 = 93%; tariff/arancel 13/15 = 87%; threat/amenaza 20/28 = 71%; military operation 7/15 = 47% (kept only as a PHRASE -- bare 'military' returns 41 non-Colombian ANDEAN hits).
DROPPED 'embajador'/'ambassador'/'embassy': 33 ANDEAN hits but only 7 Colombian (21%) -- it is Ecuadorian and Bolivian diplomacy.

DROPPED 'Petro' despite being the single strongest Colombia localizer (184 hits, zero Petrobras/Petroecuador collisions inside this gate). Only 80 of those 184 are US-related -- 43% on-topic. Petro is a COUNTRY-level anchor, and the theater carries no bundle, so he belongs to no atomic. Putting him here would have made this atomic a de-facto country gate colliding with the other two (the A2b trap).
No 'Trump' anywhere: third-party leader, hard rule 4.
'OFAC' is all-caps <=4 chars -> case-sensitive whole-word acronym path, safe.

'decertif' / 'Plan Colombia' / 'lista Clinton' measure ~0 today and are KEPT deliberately as high-precision dormant anchors -- they are the historic core of US-Colombia friction and cost nothing while quiet.

AUDIT PRUNE: dropped fr 'menace' (3 hits, 67% foreign -- it is also an English word and the non-EN path is pure substring, so it matched English 'menace' in Iran/Venezuela/Cuba titles) and ja '軍事作戦' (1 hit, 100% foreign, a Ukraine title).

bundle.term: threatened military action (phrase only -- bare 'military' leaks to Ecuador/Bolivia)
